chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented with severe respiratory distress and hypoxemia.
history: Acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) is a severe complication of COVID-19. The patient had a deficiency of alveolar surfactant, leading to severe hypoxemia. Exogenous surfactant administration was attempted to improve the overall clinical condition.
exam: Vital signs: respiratory rate 40/min, oxygen saturation 60% on room air, blood pressure 90/60 mmHg. Physical examination revealed bilateral crackles and decreased breath sounds.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: WBC 18,000/mm^3, PaO2/FiO2 ratio 100. Imaging: chest X-ray showed bilateral infiltrates. Other tests: none.
